title: Broadcom Symantec Cloud SWG Policy Analysis (Broadcom)
dataSources: Symantec WSS access logs (API-based collection via the Add-on)
spl:
index=proxy sourcetype="symantec:wss:accesslog" earliest=-7d
| where sc_filter_result="DENIED"
| stats count by cs_categories, cs_userdn, sc_filter_result
| sort -count
description: Broadcom Symantec Cloud SWG (formerly WSS) provides cloud-delivered web security with URL filtering, threat protection, and SSL inspection for all users. Tracking policy violations and threat blocks validates security posture.
value: Web security teams analyze Broadcom Symantec Cloud SWG policy enforcement by URL category and threat risk score, identifying high-risk domains accessed by users, denial trends that correlate with active campaigns, and false positive denials on uncategorized sites.
implementation: Install the Symantec WSS Add-on and configure API-based data collection. Map `cs_categories` to your acceptable-use policy. Alert when denied requests spike for a user. Report on top blocked categories and threat detections.
visualization: Bar chart (blocks by category), Table (top users blocked), Line chart (daily denied trend), Pie chart (category distribution).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Web.Web
  by Web.user, Web.url, Web.http_method span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Broadcom Symantec Web Security Service (WSS / CloudSOC) logs in `index=symantec` with `sourcetype=symantec:wss` or `sourcetype=bluecoat:proxysg:access`. Key fields: `cs-action` (ALLOWED/DENIED/TUNNELED), `cs-categories` (URL category), `cs-threat-risk` (risk score), `cs-userdn` (user DN), `cs-host`, `cs-uri`, `cs-bytes`, `sr-bytes`, `cs-method`.
• Symantec WSS (now Broadcom) is a cloud proxy/SWG that inspects web traffic, applies URL categorization, threat scanning, and DLP. Logs use W3C extended log format.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Configure WSS log export: WSS Admin > Integration > Log Export > add Splunk HEC endpoint or syslog.

Verify:
```spl
index=symantec sourcetype="symantec:wss" earliest=-1h
| stats count by cs-action
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Policy analysis:
```spl
index=symantec sourcetype="symantec:wss" cs-action=DENIED earliest=-7d
| timechart span=1d count by cs-categories limit=10
```

Understanding this SPL: Trends denied web categories over time. Symantec uses over 80 URL categories. Spikes in security-related categories (Malware, Phishing, Hacking) indicate active threats. Spikes in policy categories (Gambling, Adult) indicate enforcement. 'Uncategorized' denials may be false positives.

Threat risk analysis:
```spl
index=symantec sourcetype="symantec:wss" earliest=-24h
| where tonumber(cs-threat-risk) > 5
| stats count dc(cs-userdn) as users by cs-host, cs-threat-risk, cs-action
| sort - cs-threat-risk
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Visit a blocked category. Verify denial event.
(b) In WSS Portal: Reports > Web Activity.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Row 1 — Denial trend. Row 2 — High-risk domains. Row 3 — Top denied users.
Alert: When threat-risk > 8 domains are accessed by internal users.

Runbook:
1. **High-risk domain accessed** — Check for endpoint compromise.
2. **Denial spike** — Policy change or active campaign.
3. **Uncategorized denials** — Submit for re-categorization.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **No WSS data** — Log export may not be configured. Check WSS Integration settings.
• **`cs-categories` empty** — URL may be uncategorized in Symantec's database.
grandmaExplanation: Symantec's cloud proxy filters all our web traffic. We track which categories are being blocked and pay special attention to websites rated as high-risk — when employees visit risky sites, we check whether their devices might already be compromised.
hardware: Symantec Cloud SWG (cloud), Symantec Web Security Service Edge, Symantec Endpoint Agent
